entries.definition:
خَارِج khārij [act. part.; pl. خَارِجُون khārijūn] one coming out,
coming forth (6:122) كَمَن مَّثَلُهُ فِي الظُّلُمَاتِ لَيْسَ بِخَارِجٍ مِّنْهَا just like
someone in [deep] darkness, out of which he is not emerging.

مَخْرَج makhraj [v. n./n. of place] solution/way out (65:2) وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُ مَخْرَجًا
and whoever is mindful of God, God will find a
way out for him.

مُخْرِج mukhrij [act. part.] 1 one who brings forth (6:95) وَمُخْرِجُ
الْمَيِّتِ مِنَ الْحَيِّ and He is the one who brings out the dead from the
living 2 one who exposes, shows up, brings to light (9:64) قُلِ
اسْتَهْزِئُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ مُخْرِجٌ مَّا تَحْذَرُونَ say, ‘Carry on mocking: God will
bring to light what you fear.’

مُخْرَج mukhraj [v. n./n. of place/pass. part.; pl. مُخْرَجُون
mukhrajūn] coming out, way out, one who is brought out (17:80)
وَقُل رَّبِّ أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ وَأَخْرِجْنِي مُخْرَجَ صِدْقٍ and say, ‘My Lord, lead
me in with a true incoming and lead me out with a true outgoing.’

خَرْج kharj [n.] regular payment, tribute, dues, toll, duty
(18:94) إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰ أَن تَجْعَلَ
بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا Gog and Magog are causing destruction in the land,
so may we assign to you a tribute in return for you setting up a
barrier between us and them?

خَرَاج kharāj [n.] regular payment, dues, tribute, toll, duty
(23:72) أَمْ تَسْأَلُهُمْ خَرْجًا فَخَرَاجُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ do you [Prophet] ask them for
dues?, dues from Your Lord are best.

entries.definition:
kh–r–r sound of blowing wind, falling water or falling stones;
rumbling, snoring, purring; to enjoy affluence; to surprise; to fall,
to crumble, to collapse; to die. Of this root, خَرَّ kharra occurs 12
times in the Qur’an.

Following the uploaded instructions 

َرَّ kharra i [v. intrans.] to collapse, to fall down (7:143) وَخَرَّ مُوسَىٰ صَعِقًا and Moses collapsed unconscious; *(17:109) يَخِرُّونَ لِلْأَذْقَانِ they fall down on their faces, they prostrate themselves [lit. they fall to the chins].
